text.title: devops-platform-crossplane Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-platform-crossplane as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Exposing raw provider CRDs (e.g., rds.aws.crossplane.io/v1alpha1 DBInstance) directly to developers instead of building XRD compositions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Raw provider resources expose hundreds of configuration options. Developers will misconfigure security settings, pick oversized instances, and bypass cost controls. The composition layer is what makes Crossplane a platform tool rather than just another IaC format.

failure-mode.description: Using Crossplane as a one-shot provisioning tool without leveraging continuous reconciliation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Crossplane's primary value over Terraform is the Kubernetes reconciliation loop — it continuously corrects drift. Treating it as one-shot apply loses the drift correction benefit and leaves you with Terraform's operational model at Crossplane's operational complexity.
